memoryArtifacts.headline: The English Origin of Grace [Ravens] [Sherman] [Rogers] [Porter] and Mary [Ravens] Coolidge of Watertown, Mass. By Michael J. Wood. Pg 65-77

memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Origins Available: Dutch, English. Spelling variations of this family name include: Raven, Ravens and others. First found in Leicestershire where they were seated from very early times and were granted lands by Duke William of Normandy, their liege Lord, for their distinguished assistance at the Battle of Hastings in 1066 C.E. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Peter Raven, who settled in Virginia in 1648; Mary Raven, who settled in the Barbados in 1664; John and Jane Raven, and their four daughters, who settled in Maryland in 1672. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). This interesting name is of early medieval English origin, and developed from a nickname for a thievish or dark-haired person, from the Middle English "raven", a development of the Old English pre-7th Century "hraefn". In some cases it may be from a personal name derived from this element, taken over into Middle English from the Old Norse byname "Hrafn"; an example of this is one, Rauen de Engelbi recorded in the 1185 Pipe Rolls of Yorkshire. It is also present in place names such as Ravenscroft and Rawnsley. In England, a few early forms such as William atte Raven (London, 1344) suggest that it may also be partly derived from a house-sign or an inn-sign. Other variant spellings of the name include, Reven, Revance, Rivance, Revens, Revans and Ravens. One William Raven married Elizabeth Chalener on 27 August 1564 at St. Lawrence Jewry, London. A Coat of Arms granted to a Raven family on 15 October 1561 depicts, on a gold shield, a lion rampant double queued blue on a chief indented black three bezants. The Crest is a green demi dragon, winged red. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of William Raven, which was dated 1133, in the "Records of the Abbey of Ramsey", Bedfordshire, during the reign of King Henry 1st, known as "The Administrator", 1100 - 1135. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 – 2014.

Origin Displayed: Dutch - Origins Available: Dutch, English. Spelling variations of this family name include: Raven, Ravenn, Ravene, Ravenes, Ravenns, Ravven, Raaven, de Raven, Ravan, Ravon and many more. First found in Holland, where the name became noted for its many branches in the region, each house acquiring a status and influence which was envied by the princes of the region. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Cornelius B. Raven, who was Naturalizedc in New York in 1838; Charles Henri Raven, who came to America in 1857; and Jan Wilhelmus Raven, who settled in America in 1873. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). This interesting name is of early medieval English origin, and developed from a nickname for a thievish or dark-haired person, from the Middle English "raven", a development of the Old English pre-7th Century "hraefn". In some cases it may be from a personal name derived from this element, taken over into Middle English from the Old Norse byname "Hrafn"; an example of this is one, Rauen de Engelbi recorded in the 1185 Pipe Rolls of Yorkshire. It is also present in place names such as Ravenscroft and Rawnsley. In England, a few early forms such as William atte Raven (London, 1344) suggest that it may also be partly derived from a house-sign or an inn-sign. Other variant spellings of the name include, Reven, Revance, Rivance, Revens, Revans and Ravens. One William Raven married Elizabeth Chalener on 27 August 1564 at St. Lawrence Jewry, London. A Coat of Arms granted to a Raven family on 15 October 1561 depicts, on a gold shield, a lion rampant double queued blue on a chief indented black three bezants. The Crest is a green demi dragon, winged red. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of William Raven, which was dated 1133, in the "Records of the Abbey of Ramsey", Bedfordshire, during the reign of King Henry 1st, known as "The Administrator", 1100 - 1135. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling. zh.lifeSummary: 當William Ravens於1519年出生在英國艾可迪德哈姆時，他的父親Richard Raven25歲，母親Alyse Carre Doggett17歲。他於1544年5月7日之前與Joan Starlyng在英國艾可迪德哈姆結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1580年8月1日在他的家鄉享年61歲。

nameMeanings.name: Ravens
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London): variant of Raven , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Raven
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Raven (Old Norse Hrafn, Old English Hræfn, both meaning ‘raven’) or else a nickname from this word, probably denoting a dark-haired man.

English: occasionally perhaps also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn distinguished by the sign of a raven (see 1 above). Surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English.
North German and Dutch: from Middle Low German rave(n) ‘raven’, a nickname or an old personal name. Compare 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When William Ravens was born in 1519, in Dedham, Essex, England, his father, Richard Raven, was 25 and his mother, Alyse Carre Doggett, was 17. He married Joan Starlyng before 7 May 1544, in Dedham, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 1 August 1580, in his hometown, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Ravens est né en 1519, à Dedham, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Richard Raven, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Alyse Carre Doggett, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Joan Starlyng avant 7 mai 1544, à Dedham, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 1 août 1580, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Ravens 1519 in Dedham, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Raven 25 und seine Mutter Alyse Carre Doggett 17. Er hat vor 7. Mai 1544 in Dedham, Essex, England Joan Starlyng geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 1. August 1580 im Alter von 61 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Ravens è nato il nel 1519 nel Dedham, Essex, England, il suo padre, Richard Raven, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Alyse Carre Doggett, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Joan Starlyng prima del 7 maggio 1544, nel Dedham, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 1 agosto 1580, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 61 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: William Ravens(이)가 1519년 영국 에세스 데드함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Raven(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Alyse Carre Doggett(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1544년 5월 7일 이전 영국 에세스 데드함에서 Joan Starlyng(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1580년 8월 1일 그의 고향에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Ravens nasceu em 1519, em Dedham, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Raven, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Alyse Carre Doggett, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Starlyng antes 7 de maio de 1544, em Dedham, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1 de agosto de 1580, em sua cidade natal, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Ravens родился в 1519 г. в местности дедхам, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Raven было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Alyse Carre Doggett, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Starlyng перед 7 май 1544 г. в местности дедхам, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 1 августа 1580 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Ravens nació en 1519, en Dedham, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Raven, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Alyse Carre Doggett, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Joan Starlyng antes de 7 de mayo de 1544, en Dedham, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 1 de agosto de 1580, en su pueblo, a la edad de 61 años.
